text.title: Customer Testimonials and Social Proof Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Customer Testimonials and Social Proof application MUST enforce, plus a skip-this-methodology fallback. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Every testimonial MUST carry full name + role + company + real photo. Initials-only or stock-photo testimonials halve conversion impact and are rejected.
rule.rationale: Credibility signals are cumulative; anonymous attribution reduces believability regardless of quote quality.

rule.statement: Asks MUST fire immediately after a measurable customer win, not at fixed time intervals. Asks at week-N are rejected.
rule.rationale: Peak satisfaction is time-limited; users 3 weeks post-win are no longer emotionally connected.

rule.statement: ≥1 testimonial MUST sit within visual proximity (above-fold or adjacent) of every primary CTA on the landing page. Bottom-only placement is rejected.
rule.rationale: Testimonials reduce purchase anxiety at the decision moment; bottom-only placement helps only the already-decided.

rule.statement: Quotes MUST contain a specific result (number, before→after, or named outcome). Generic praise ('great product') is stripped.
rule.rationale: Result-specific quotes lift CVR measurably; generic praise has near-zero conversion impact.

rule.statement: Every testimonial MUST carry a refresh_date ≤ 12 months old at publication time. Older quotes are flagged stale and pulled.
rule.rationale: Stale testimonials signal an inactive customer base; prospects notice date drift.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition / triggered skip, and route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Methodology hygiene: mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
